bindings.title: Tokyo Listening Bar sign
bindings.excerpt: The room-defining sign ties the scene to Heisei-era left-field pop and CD-age store identity.
bindings.source_summary: A compilation framing Japanese left-field pop from the CD age as a shop-floor lineage of labels, curation, and physical media.
bindings.source_image_alt: Screenshot of the Heisei No Oto Music From Memory page

bindings.title: CRT listening station
bindings.excerpt: The listening station opens the NTS guide mix that maps the CD-era Japanese electronica lineage.
bindings.source_title: Late 90s / early 00s Japanese ambient, techno and electronica guide
bindings.source_summary: An NTS-linked guide mix connecting ambient, IDM, glitch, and left-field electronica into a coherent CD-age browsing world.
bindings.source_image_alt: YouTube thumbnail for the Japanese ambient techno and electronica guide

bindings.title: Genre shelf labels
bindings.excerpt: These shelf headers index the listening-techno and ambient crosscurrents documented by Virtual Dreams II.
bindings.source_summary: A compilation and liner-note context for Japanese listening techno, IDM, ambient, and CD-first club culture in the 1990s.
bindings.source_image_alt: Screenshot of the Virtual Dreams II Music From Memory page

bindings.title: Recommendation grid
bindings.excerpt: The handwritten recommendation wall echoes album-by-album collector guidance from Takako Ishiguro’s CD-era release.
bindings.source_title: Takako Ishiguro - Thank You For Your Hard Work (1997)
bindings.source_summary: A specific late-1990s album signal grounding the listening bar in jewel-case culture, recommendation slips, and small-scene curation.
bindings.source_image_alt: YouTube thumbnail for Takako Ishiguro - Thank You For Your Hard Work

bindings.title: Spectrum poster
bindings.excerpt: The event poster extends the room backward into the earlier environmental and ambient lineage that still shapes this shelf logic.
bindings.source_summary: A foundational Japanese ambient compilation that feeds the quieter shelf and flyer logic behind the listening bar world.
bindings.source_image_alt: Screenshot of the Kankyo Ongaku Music From Memory page

bindings.title: Opiate Objects display
bindings.excerpt: The featured-counter display channels the collector-facing presentation style of Heisei no Oto and adjacent CD-era compilations.
bindings.source_summary: A tactile compilation page where typography, sleeves, and genre adjacency matter as much as the tracklist.
bindings.source_image_alt: Screenshot of the Heisei No Oto Music From Memory page

bindings.title: Open CD binders
bindings.excerpt: The open binders act like a hands-on browse state for the NTS guide’s network of labels and compilations.
bindings.source_title: Japanese ambient, techno and electronica guide
bindings.source_summary: A guide mix that works as an audio browse path through the same family of CDs spread open in the binders.
bindings.source_image_alt: YouTube thumbnail for the Japanese ambient techno and electronica guide

bindings.title: Compilation shelf tags
bindings.excerpt: The shelf tags make explicit the compilation logic documented by Virtual Dreams II and its CD-first club culture lineage.
bindings.source_summary: A release page tying Japanese listening techno to labels, compilations, and CD-centric ambient techno circulation.
bindings.source_image_alt: Screenshot of the Virtual Dreams II Music From Memory page
